binaries.title: bthport.sys - feature-staged refactor of the HCI LE advertising-request coordinator teardown

changed_functions.name: MarkingAllAdvertisingRequestAsNewEntry (0x1C0074D10) — HciLELegacyAdvertisingRequestCoordinator state-machine entry (unpatched at 0x1C0074D10, patched at 0x1C0074D60)
key_changes: Extracts the pre-patch inline read-clear-validate-and-decrement of the IRP cancel routine into the new helper ClearRequestCancellationRoutine (0x1C0072854), which reproduces the inline block instruction-for-instruction: atomic xchg read-and-clear at +0x68, comparison against OnAdvertisementRequestCancelThunk (0x1C00719E0) and OnPendingAdvertisementRequestCancelThunk (0x1C00718A0), and lock xadd decrement of the operation-guard reference at Event+0x18 with KeSetEvent on reaching zero

Places the helper call behind the new WIL feature-staging gate Feature_1166795066__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage (0x1C0075A04); this flag has 0 occurrences in the unpatched image and 3 in the patched image
Retains the pre-patch behaviour in the feature-disabled (else) branch at 0x1C0074E4A, so with the flag off the patched driver behaves as the unpatched driver

The only behavioural delta in the enabled branch is that the request cancelled flag (bit 0x4) is not set when the cancel-routine field was already NULL; the coordinator spinlock, the atomic cancel-routine handoff, and the guard-reference acquire/release balance are unchanged
changed_functions.security_notes: Not a security fix. In both builds MarkingAllAdvertisingRequestAsNewEntry walks the pending advertising-request list under the coordinator spinlock (KeAcquireSpinLockRaiseToDpc on coordinator+0x10, held across the whole walk). The IRP cancel routine OnAdvertisementRequestCancelThunk (0x1C00719E0) acquires the SAME coordinator spinlock (lea rsi,[rdi+10h]; KeAcquireSpinLockRaiseToDpc at 0x1C0071A6C) before it unlinks the request and decrements the operation-guard reference (0x1C0071AF2), so the teardown and the cancel path are serialized and cannot race. The atomic xchg on the cancel-routine field at +0x68 is the standard IoSetCancelRoutine-style handoff and is identical in both builds. The recognized-thunk guard decrement moved verbatim into ClearRequestCancellationRoutine; the guard is acquired once and released at most once per iteration in the unpatched build and in both patched branches. There is no reference-count imbalance, no dangling pointer, and no reachable memory-safety primitive. The change is a code extraction gated behind a WIL feature-staging flag with the pre-patch path retained in the else branch = staged rollout / servicing churn.
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
UNPATCHED 0x1C0074D10 inline teardown:
0x1C0074D3A: call    __imp_KeAcquireSpinLockRaiseToDpc ; coordinator spinlock
0x1C0074DD7: xor     eax, eax
0x1C0074DD9: xchg    rax, [rdi-40h]            ; read-and-clear cancel routine at +0x68
0x1C0074DDD: test    rax, rax
0x1C0074DE0: jz      0x1C0074E33               ; NULL -> set cancelled flag
0x1C0074DE2: lea     rcx, OnAdvertisementRequestCancelThunk (0x1C00719E0)
0x1C0074DE9: cmp     rax, rcx
0x1C0074DEC: jz      0x1C0074DF3
0x1C0074DEE: cmp     rax, rdx                  ; OnPendingAdvertisementRequestCancelThunk (0x1C00718A0)
0x1C0074DF1: jnz     0x1C0074E1B
0x1C0074DF3: or      eax, 0FFFFFFFFh
0x1C0074DF6: lock xadd [rbp+18h], eax         ; decrement operation-guard reference
0x1C0074E08: call    __imp_KeSetEvent
0x1C0074E1B: mov     rax, rdx
0x1C0074E1E: xchg    rax, [rdi-40h]           ; re-install pending cancel thunk
0x1C0074E22: cmp     byte ptr [rdi-64h], 0    ; byte flag at +0x44
0x1C0074E4F: call    release_operation_guard_reference

PATCHED 0x1C0074D60 (feature-gated, same work):
0x1C0074DDF: lock inc dword ptr [rbx+18h]     ; acquire operation guard (unchanged)
0x1C0074E0C: call    Feature_1166795066__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage (0x1C0075A04) ; NEW gate
0x1C0074E17: test    eax, eax
0x1C0074E19: jz      0x1C0074E4A              ; disabled -> legacy branch (retained)
0x1C0074E1B: call    ClearRequestCancellationRoutine (0x1C0072854) ; enabled branch
0x1C0074E20: test    al, al
0x1C0074E22: jz      0x1C0074E80
0x1C0074E2B: xchg    rax, [rdi+68h]           ; re-install pending cancel thunk
0x1C0074E4A: call    ClearRequestCancellationRoutine (0x1C0072854) ; legacy branch
0x1C0074E4F: test    al, al
0x1C0074E51: jz      0x1C0074E6F              ; not recognized -> set cancelled flag
0x1C0074E88: call    release_operation_guard_reference

ClearRequestCancellationRoutine (0x1C0072854) reproduces the inline block:
0x1C0072854: sub     rsp, 28h
0x1C007285A: xchg    rax, [rdx+68h]           ; atomic read-and-clear
0x1C007285E: test    rax, rax
0x1C0072861: jz      0x1C00728A2              ; NULL -> return 0
0x1C0072863: lea     rdx, OnAdvertisementRequestCancelThunk (0x1C00719E0)
0x1C007286D: jz      0x1C007287B
0x1C007286F: lea     rdx, OnPendingAdvertisementRequestCancelThunk (0x1C00718A0)
0x1C0072879: jnz     0x1C00728A0              ; unrecognized non-NULL -> return 1, no decrement
0x1C007287B: add     rcx, 0F0h                ; Event
0x1C0072885: lock xadd [rcx+18h], eax         ; decrement guard reference (recognized thunk only)
0x1C0072894: call    __imp_KeSetEvent
0x1C00728A0: mov     al, 1                    ; return 1 (non-NULL cancel routine)
0x1C00728A6: retn

Cancel routine shares the coordinator spinlock (refutes any race):
OnAdvertisementRequestCancelThunk (0x1C00719E0):
0x1C00719F5: call    __imp_IoReleaseCancelSpinLock
0x1C0071A6F: call    __imp_KeAcquireSpinLockRaiseToDpc ; SAME lock (coordinator+0x10) the teardown holds
0x1C0071AF2: lock xadd [rcx+18h], eax          ; operation-guard decrement, serialized

changed_functions.name: MarkingAllAdvertisingRequestAsNewEntry (0x1C0079990) — HciLEExtendedAdvertisingRequestCoordinator state-machine entry (unpatched at 0x1C0079990, patched relocated to 0x1C0079AB0)
key_changes: Same feature-staging change as the Legacy entry, applied to the Extended coordinator's copy of the generated state entry

The Extended coordinator already factored the teardown into ClearRequestCancellationRoutine (unpatched 0x1C0076D38, relocated to 0x1C0076E58); the only change is the added Feature_1166795066__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage (0x1C0075A04) gate at 0x1C0079B64 splitting the existing helper call into an enabled branch (0x1C0079B73) and a retained legacy branch (0x1C0079BA2)
Helper logic and the recognized cancel thunks are unchanged; the coordinator spinlock and guard-reference accounting are unchanged
changed_functions.security_notes: Not a security fix, for the same reasons as the Legacy entry. The Extended teardown runs under the coordinator spinlock and the Extended cancel routine acquires the same lock before manipulating the request and the operation-guard reference. The patch only adds the WIL feature-staging gate around an already-existing helper, retaining the pre-patch behaviour in the else branch. No behavioural change to locking or reference-count balance.
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
UNPATCHED 0x1C0079990 teardown block:
0x1C0079A46: call    ClearRequestCancellationRoutine (0x1C0076D38) ; no feature gate
0x1C0079A4F: lea     rax, OnPendingAdvertisementRequestCancelThunk
0x1C0079A56: xchg    rax, [rsi+68h]           ; re-install pending cancel thunk

PATCHED 0x1C0079AB0 equivalent:
0x1C0079B64: call    Feature_1166795066__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage (0x1C0075A04) ; NEW gate
0x1C0079B71: jz      0x1C0079BA2              ; disabled -> legacy branch (retained)
0x1C0079B73: call    ClearRequestCancellationRoutine (0x1C0076E58) ; enabled branch
0x1C0079BA2: call    ClearRequestCancellationRoutine (0x1C0076E58) ; legacy branch

ClearRequestCancellationRoutine unchanged in logic (unpatched 0x1C0076D38 relocated to patched 0x1C0076E58): atomic read-and-clear at +0x68, thunk comparison, guard-reference decrement for a recognized thunk.

changed_functions.name: wil_details_IsEnabledFallback (0x1C00F1474) / wil_details_IsEnabledFallback (0x1C0075954) — WIL feature-staging fallback
key_changes: Modified signature to receive a feature-state data pointer as a parameter instead of referencing a hardcoded global
Register allocation changes to carry the new parameter; supports the new feature-staging flag
changed_functions.security_notes: WIL feature-staging plumbing supporting Feature_1166795066__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage. Not security-relevant.

changed_functions.name: Feature_2327586105__private_IsEnabledFallback (0x1C00F1510) / Feature_2327586105__private_IsEnabledFallback (0x1C00F15D4) — WIL feature-staging fallback wrapper
key_changes: Updated to thread the new feature-state pointer as the third parameter to wil_details_IsEnabledFallback
Trivial wrapper change to accommodate the new parameter

summary: No security-relevant change. The patch refactors the HCI LE advertising-request coordinator teardown state entry MarkingAllAdvertisingRequestAsNewEntry for both the Legacy coordinator (0x1C0074D10, patched 0x1C0074D60) and the Extended coordinator (0x1C0079990, patched relocated 0x1C0079AB0), and threads two WIL feature-staging fallback helpers. The reworked teardown is placed behind a new WIL feature-staging flag, Feature_1166795066__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage (0x1C0075A04; 0 occurrences in the unpatched image, 3 in the patched), with the pre-patch behaviour retained in the feature-disabled (else) branch. For the Legacy coordinator the change extracts the pre-patch inline read-clear-validate-and-decrement of the IRP cancel routine into a new helper, ClearRequestCancellationRoutine (0x1C0072854), that reproduces the inline block instruction-for-instruction (atomic xchg at +0x68, comparison against OnAdvertisementRequestCancelThunk 0x1C00719E0 and OnPendingAdvertisementRequestCancelThunk 0x1C00718A0, and lock xadd decrement of the operation-guard reference at Event+0x18). For the Extended coordinator the helper already existed (0x1C0076D38 relocated to 0x1C0076E58) and only the gate was added. The originally reported race / reference-count imbalance / use-after-free is not supported: the teardown holds the coordinator spinlock (coordinator+0x10) across the whole list walk, and the IRP cancel routine OnAdvertisementRequestCancelThunk (0x1C00719E0) acquires the SAME lock (0x1C0071A6C) before it unlinks the request and decrements the guard reference (0x1C0071AF2), so the two paths are serialized. The atomic cancel-routine handoff is the standard IoSetCancelRoutine idiom and is identical in both builds, and the guard-reference acquire/release balance is unchanged. This is staged-rollout / servicing churn, not a memory-safety fix; no CWE applies and no exploit primitive is demonstrable.
